Computational biology infrastructure in 30 minutes, not 6 months

bioAF is a free, open source platform that gives small biotech teams production-grade pipelines, notebooks, and experiment tracking — no infrastructure engineer required.

Get Started See How It Works

Infrastructure shouldn't be the bottleneck

Small biotech teams lose months to problems that have nothing to do with science.

👥

The Hiring Gap

You need a DevOps engineer to set up pipelines, notebooks, and storage — but you can't find one, or can't justify the cost for a 10-person team.

⏳

The Time Cost

Setting up compute infrastructure from scratch takes 2–6 months. That's half a year before your first pipeline run.

🔍

The Metadata Gap

Results live in one place, sample metadata in another, and the connection between them exists only in someone's head.

Built for your whole team

Everyone from the bench to the boardroom gets what they need.

🧬

Bioinformaticians

Run scRNA-seq pipelines, launch Jupyter or RStudio with versioned environments, and access your data — all from one place.

🧪

Bench Scientists

Register experiments, upload FASTQ files, and explore results with interactive visualizations. No command line needed.

📊

PIs & Lab Managers

Track team progress, monitor cloud costs, and maintain a complete audit trail from sample to publication.

From zero to science in four steps

1

Deploy

Clone the repo and run one command. bioAF sets up everything you need on your Google Cloud project.

i What happens under the hood?
bioAF uses Terraform to provision a Kubernetes cluster, managed database, and storage buckets on your GCP project. You don't need to know what any of that means — the setup command handles it all.
2

Register

Create experiments with structured metadata and add your samples. bioAF tracks everything from bench to results.

3

Run

Launch pipelines from the built-in catalog or start a notebook session. Monitor progress in real time.

i What pipelines are included?
bioAF ships with nf-core pipelines for scRNA-seq, bulk RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and more. You can also add your own custom Nextflow or Snakemake workflows.
4

Explore

View QC dashboards, browse results in cellxgene, and share findings with your team.

Everything you need, nothing you don't

A complete platform for computational biology at small biotech scale.

Pipeline Execution

Run Nextflow and Snakemake workflows from a built-in catalog with real-time monitoring and DAG visualization.

Notebook Sessions

Launch JupyterHub or RStudio with versioned environments and automatic idle shutdown.

Experiment Tracking

MINSEQE-compliant metadata, sample batches, and a full status lifecycle from registration to completion.

Interactive Visualization

Explore single-cell data in cellxgene, review QC dashboards, and search the plot archive.

Cost Management

See per-project cloud costs, set budget alerts, and track spending trends over time.

Audit & Compliance

An immutable audit trail with full provenance — who did what, when, and why.

Data Management

Upload, browse, and download files with automatic lifecycle management across storage tiers.

Infrastructure Management

Enable or disable platform components from the UI. See cost estimates before you provision anything.

Auto-Ingest

Connect your sequencer and let bioAF automatically detect and import new FASTQ files as they arrive.

Free and open source. Always.

bioAF is open source software. Deploy it on your own cloud project and own your data, your infrastructure, and your results. No vendor lock-in, no per-seat fees.

Get Started

Free and open source computational biology infrastructure.
